title: Indulgent Smoky Japanese Pork Gyoza
description: Satisfying, smoky pork gyoza packed with flavor and low‑carb goodness, perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: Press pork into a bowl and add salt, pepper, sesame oil, soy sauce, mirin, minced garlic, grated ginger, chopped scallions, and ground black pepper; mix until well combined.
Fold in shredded napa cabbage and toasted sesame seeds; mix for 2 minutes until the mixture is moist.
Place a low-carb gyoza wrapper on a clean surface, spoon 1 tablespoon of filling onto the center, and press the edges together to seal tightly.
Repeat until all wrappers are filled.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add 1 tsp of sesame oil; place gyoza in a single layer, skin side down.
Sauté until the bottom is golden brown, about 3 minutes, then pour 1/4 cup of water into the pan.
Cover the skillet and let the gyoza steam for 5 minutes; remove the lid when the water has evaporated.
Finish by drizzling a smoky glaze made of 2 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1/4 tsp smoked paprika, and a dash of garlic powder over the cooked gyoza; serve hot.
